title: Count Square Submatrices with All Ones
hints: Count individual 1s first, then look for larger squares. A square of size k contains smaller squares of size k-1 within it.
Use dynamic programming where the state at each cell represents the side length of the largest square ending there.
For a cell with 1, its max square side depends on the three neighboring cells (left, top, top-left). Sum all resulting side lengths.
